new_classes.label_en: Other non-alcoholic beverage, incl. tonic preparations (subheading 2202 99 19)
new_classes.label_de: Andere nicht alkoholhaltige Getränke, einschließlich Anregungsmittel (Tonika) (Unterposition 2202 99 19)
new_classes.definition_en: Non-alcoholic beverage classified under CN subheading 2202 99 19, including tonic preparations (Tonika) as described in the explanatory note to Chapter 22. These beverages may be based on extracts from plants (including herbs), and may contain added vitamins and/or minerals. They are often referred to as food supplements but are presented as beverages for direct consumption.
A material entity that is an extract derived from plants or herbs, used as an ingredient or base in beverages or tonic preparations classified under Chapter 22.
A material entity representing added vitamins and/or minerals present as additives in non-alcoholic beverages or tonic preparations classified under CN heading 2202.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the principal distinguishing criteria from the legal text: (1) non-alcoholic character (via NonAlcoholicBeverageQuality restriction), (2) plant/herbal extract basis (via PlantExtractIngredient part restriction), and (3) optional vitamin/mineral additives (via VitaminOrMineralAdditive part restriction). The class is placed as a subclass of NonAlcoholicFlavouredBeverage2202 to reflect the heading 2202 hierarchy and the "other" (2202 99) branch. A gap remains: the legal note references the Chapter 22 explanatory note (second paragraph) defining tonic preparations more precisely, including the criterion that these beverages must be presented for direct consumption and not for skin care use — the NotForSkinCareUseQuality class exists in the TBox and could be used but is not explicitly mandated by the legal note text for this specific subheading. Additionally, the vitamin/mineral additive restriction is modelled as an existential (someValuesFrom) making it a necessary feature; the legal text says these MAY be present, so this slightly over-constrains the class. The vitamin/mineral restriction is therefore listed but should be understood as characterising, not definitional. The "food supplement" designation is editorial and not directly modelled. Overall coverage is good for structural classification purposes.
